preview_text:
The model favours England strongly, assigning an 87% win probability against a 0% chance for Ghana. The underdog's only viable path is a draw, which the model rates at 13%, implying the model sees this as a heavily tilted contest in England's favour.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects a pronounced asymmetry. England's overwhelming modelled edge leaves little room for competitive balance; Ghana's near-zero win probability suggests the model expects few realistic routes to three points. A draw remains the sole realistic upset scenario, though even that sits well below even odds. This is a match the model frames as distinctly one-sided.

The market consensus diverges sharply from the model. Sportsbooks assign Ghana a 10% win probability—substantially higher than the model's 0%—and shade England down to 72%, a gap of 15 percentage points. Polymarket prices show extreme uncertainty, splitting all outcomes evenly at 33% each. The dashboard makes no claim about which view is correct; the divergence is material and suggests meaningful disagreement about Ghana's true winning chances on a neutral pitch.
